jobs.title: End User Computing Engineer (Onsite)
jobs.summary: Overview: We have an exciting End User Computing Engineer Opportunity for an experienced professional to join our team at Pediatrix Medical Group. We are a national organization, and one of the nation’s largest providers of prenatal, neonatal and...
jobs.description:
Overview:

We have an exciting End User Computing Engineer Opportunity for an experienced professional to join our team at Pediatrix Medical Group. We are a national organization, and one of the nation’s largest providers of prenatal, neonatal and pediatric services.

Talented business professionals from diverse backgrounds choose Pediatrix because we are an exciting and innovative company that focuses on a team approach to improve the lives of patients everywhere. We are confident that you'll love being a part of the Pediatrix team.
Responsibilities:

The End User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for the overall lifecycle (plan, build, run) of all enterprise endpoints including laptops, desktops and mobile devices. Develops and maintains system images, application packages and patch management for all endpoints in the enterprise. Partners with other key engineering teams and security to ensure strategy and architecture support the overall IT strategy and key system integrations. You will be a recognized Subject Matter Expert and manage large projects and processes with suitable support. You will be willing to work with colleagues to improve the skills of others in the team. Your outputs are likely to have a broad impact on the business with high visibility.

Design, develop & maintain all system images for laptops, desktops & mobile devices.
Certify all new hardware and software for endpoints in the environment prior to purchase or release.
Leverage modern industry leading deployment technologies for overall endpoint management that also optimizes the employee experience
Updates and patches all OS & application patches and vulnerabilities
Creates and maintains application packages developing a software store for employees to download required software
Ensures all endpoints meet security requirements
Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) administration
Design and maintain Microsoft Azure AD management as it impacts the endpoint space
Audits and analyzes data to ensure processes are followed and standards are maintained
Provides tier 3 desktop support
Works independently on larger, moderately complex projects/ assignments.
Responsible for making adjustments or recommended enhancements to systems and processes to solve problems or improve effectiveness of job area.
Problems and issues faced are general and may require understanding of broader set of issues but typically are not complex.
Works on problems that may require understanding of other job areas.
May provide guidance and assistance to entry level professionals and/or support employees.
Qualifications:

Education/Experience:

2 years’ experience in the end user compute space
Experience in endpoint management: install, support, configure, evaluate, maintain, monitor, and analyze systems and software in both mobile and PC devices.
Experience with application upgrades, security patching, and platform updates
Strong experience with Microsoft Autopilot, Intune/endpoint manager

Knowledge/Skills:

Understanding of hardware and software dependencies
Ability to translate business requirements to technical requirements
Strong team player who can work across multiple functional areas and disciplines with attention to detail
Benefits and Compensation:

Take great care of the patient, every day and every way.TM At Pediatrix & Obstetrix, that’s not only our motto at work each day; it’s also how we view our employees and their families. We know that our greatest asset is YOU.

We take pride in offering comprehensive benefits in a vast array of plans that fit your life and lifestyle, supporting your health and overall well-being. Benefits offered include, but are not limited to: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, Disability, Healthcare FSA, Dependent Care FSA and HSAs, as well as a 401k plan and Employee Stock Purchase Program. Some benefits are provided at no cost, while others require a cost share between employees and the company. Employees may also select voluntary plans and pay for these benefits through convenient payroll deductions. Our benefit programs are just one of the many ways Pediatrix & Obstetrix helps our employees take care of themselves and their families.
About Us:

Pediatrix Medical Group is one of the nation’s leading providers of highly specialized health care for women, babies and children. Since 1979, Pediatrix has grown from a single neonatology practice to a national, multi-specialty medical group. Pediatrix-affiliated clinicians are committed to providing coordinated, compassionate and clinically excellent services to women, babies and children across the continuum of care, both in hospital settings and office-based practices. The group’s high-quality, evidence-based care is bolstered by significant investments in research, education, quality-improvement and safety initiatives.

Please Note: Fraudulent job postings/job scams are becoming increasingly common. All genuine Pediatrix job postings can be found through the Pediatrix Careers site: www.pediatrix.com/careers.

#LI-Remote

#PedCorp
Pediatrix is an Equal Opportunity Employer:

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

jobs.description:
Job Summary

Job Description

At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place.

We are seeking a Team Lead: Digital Workplace Engineer to join the firm. The Team Lead: Digital Workplace Engineer oversees user experience, performance, and reliability of all digital workplace services firmwide. This position provides strategic, technical, and operational leadership for endpoint engineering, ensuring improved productivity, service quality, and user experience outcomes. The role sets direction, governance, and standards for workplace technologies, driving excellence and alignment across Engineering, Service Desk, and Infrastructure teams. Responsibilities include eliminating user experience friction, reducing recurring issues, and delivering scalable, consistent global services. This is achieved through automation, standardization, and disciplined execution to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency.

KEY RELATIONSHIPS

Cloud Platform Engineering, Enterprise Applications, Security, Global Support Services, Deskside Support, Network & Collaboration, Business Stakeholders

Essential Duties

Owning the entire digital workplace user experience while driving continuous improvement Establishing metrics for DEX and monitoring user sentiment to ensure alignment with objectives and enhance overall experience. Defining standard operating procedures and RACI models while ensuring accountability is upheld consistently across all relevant areas. Conducting root cause analysis and removing recurring issues. Partnering with the Service Desk to minimize incidents while enhancing resolution efficiency and effectiveness. Leading change initiatives and driving strategies to enhance user adoption effectively. Standardizing endpoint configurations while reducing complexity Defining key performance indicators and delivering reports tailored for senior-level audiences, ensuring clarity and actionable insights. Owning escalation frameworks and incident responses Leading vendor performance aligned with experience service level agreements. Driving the adoption of practical artificial intelligence solutions aligned with enhancing productivity. Leading and developing the team while focusing on achieving results-oriented expectations.

Experience Skills Required

Demonstrate proficiency in Windows, endpoint management, Azure, and automation while ensuring effective implementation and seamless operational functionality. Demonstrate exceptional stakeholder influence and communication skills 7+ years in digital workplace / endpoint engineering Possess experience improving user experience (DEX) and service outcomes Demonstrate proven track record partnering with Service Desk organizations Possess experience driving automation, standardization, and process maturity Possess experience leading change management and adoption programs Demonstrate exceptional KPI definition and performance reporting experience

Compensation Salary $128,000 - $249,000

The compensation salary for this position will be determined during the interview process and will vary based on multiple factors, including but not limited to prior experience, relevant expertise, current business needs, and market factors.

About The Firm

K&L Gates is a fully integrated global law firm with lawyers located across five continents in more than 40 offices. We have experienced dramatic growth in the past decade and now rank among the largest U.S. based law firms in the world. We take pride in constantly striving for innovation, imagination and an entrepreneurial spirit. We come up with big ideas and then roll up our sleeves to get the job done, guiding our clients through their most complex issues in a variety of industry sectors and across multiple regions of the world.

The industry recognition the firm has garnered emanates from the foundation of a global community aligned on behalf of our clients. The people at K&L Gates are committed to working together to create a legacy for each other, the firm, our clients, and the communities in which we serve. We thrive in an inclusive and socially conscious environment that embraces diversity and takes a holistic approach to the career evolution of all our professionals.

For more information or to view other job opportunities, please click here to go back to our careers page.

Notice: We participate in E-Verify in certain Firm locations for purposes of verifying employment eligibility.

Please be advised that this position requires successful completion of a background check. In accordance with the Washington State Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance, San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the California Fair Chance Act, and applicable law qualified California and Washington State applicants with criminal history records will be considered. A criminal history will not categorically exclude a candidate from employment, and the Firm will consider relevant factors before making employment decisions based on criminal history information.

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Benefits

K&L Gates offers our personnel a comprehensive suite of benefits to help meet your needs now and in the future. Depending on your eligibility, options for full-time personnel include:

Medical/Prescription Drug Coverage (including a Health Savings Account feature) Back-up Child/Elder Care and access to a caregiving concierge Dental Insurance Wellness Program Vision Insurance Pre-tax Commuting Benefits 401(k) Retirement Plan and Profit Sharing Business Travel Accident Insurance Short- and Long-term Disability Protection Pet Insurance Life Insurance (including Basic, Supplemental, Spouse, Child, and Accidental Death and Dismemberment) Health Advocacy Services Paid Time Off (25-30 days per year) Identity Protection/Restoration and Fraud Insurance Parental Leave (18 weeks of which 6 are paid; short-term disability may provide additional paid time off) Student loan refinancing options and access to a student loan concierge service Paid Holidays (12) Addiction Resources Family Building Benefits Breast Milk Delivery and Lactation Support Services Flexible Spending Accounts Employees also may be eligible to receive bonuses and certain expense reimbursements Employee Assistance Program Professional Development and CLE Credit Opportunities 529 Deductions Relocation Accident Insurance Employee Referral Program Critical Illness Insurance Hybrid/Remote Work Opportunities Hospital Indemnity Insurance Perks including: Technology, Entertainment, and Travel Discount Programs Bereavement Leave All other benefits (such as leaves of absence) required by law

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

The Firm is an equal opportunity employer. It is the policy of the Firm that employment decisions shall be based on merit, qualifications and competence. Employment practices will not be influenced or affected by virtue of a person’s race (including traits associated with race, including hair texture and protective hairstyles such as afros, braids, locks, and twists), religion, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, gender, parental status, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, familial status, domestic violence victim status, military and veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. This policy governs all aspects of employment including, without limitation, recruiting, hiring, compensation, benefits, promotion, assignment, and dismissal. In addition, it is the Firm’s policy to provide an environment that is free of prohibited harassment of any kind including, without limitation, that which is based on sex, race, age, disability, ethnic background, or any other category protected by law (including the categories listed above). The Firm complies with federal and state disability laws and makes reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees with disabilities. If you require reasonable accommodation in completing this application, interviewing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, please contact askHR@klgates.com .

jobs.description:
This job is with Mayer Brown International LLP, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.

Overview

Mayer Brown is an international law firm positioned to represent the world's major corporations, funds, and financial institutions in their most important and complex transactions and disputes. We are recognized by our clients as strategic partners with deep commercial instincts and a commitment to creatively anticipating their needs and delivering excellence in everything we do.

We are a collegial, collaborative firm where highly motivated individuals with an unwavering commitment to excellence receive the opportunity, support, and development they need to grow, thrive, and realize their greatest potential all while supporting the Firm's client service principles of excellence, strategic partnership, commercial instinct, integrated strengths, innovation, and collaboration across our international firm.

If you enjoy working with team members whose defining characteristics are exceptional client service, initiative, professionalism, responsiveness, and adaptability, you may be the person we are seeking to join our Information Technology department in our Chicago office, as a Assistant Director: Digital Workplace and Productivity Platforms.

The Assistant Director: Digital Workplace and Productivity Platforms is a senior technology leader responsible for the strategy, transformation, engineering, and lifecycle management of the Firm's end-user computing, desktop applications, endpoint platforms, and digital workplace services globally. This role will lead the ongoing transformation of the Firm's desktop technology and end-user services to cloud-based, automated, and agile operating models, partnering closely with Infrastructure, Security, Data, Platforms, and Support leadership to deliver secure, stable, and modern user experiences at scale. The role is accountable for building a high-performing organization, driving measurable service improvement, and ensuring the Firm's user-facing technology estate evolves in line with business needs and enterprise architecture.

Responsibilities

Essential Functions:

Digital Workplace Strategy & Transformation

Define and execute a global digital workplace and desktop applications strategy aligned with Firm priorities, enterprise architecture, and user needsLead the continued transformation of end-user computing, application delivery, and workplace services toward cloud-based, automated, and agile operating modelsEstablish roadmaps, priorities, governance, and success measures for strategic initiatives across endpoint, desktop applications, workplace experience, and service modernizationEndpoint, Application & Cloud Engineering

Own the global endpoint environment, including Windows endpoints, mobile platforms, endpoint lifecycle standards, and enterprise desktop application deliveryOversee modern endpoint management capabilities, including Intune, device management, software deployment, and lifecycle automationLead virtual workspace and cloud-based end-user computing platforms, including Azure Virtual Desktop, Windows 365, and related Microsoft technologies, as appropriateCross-Functional Technology Leadership

Partner closely with Infrastructure, Security, Data, Platforms, Service Management, and Support leaders to define and deliver an integrated end-user technology roadmapEnsure user-facing technologies are aligned to enterprise standards for architecture, security, observability, automation, supportability, and resilienceAct as a senior stakeholder and collaborator on cross-functional initiatives that impact endpoint, identity, access, productivity, application delivery, and workplace experienceAutomation, AI & Service Improvement

Drive automation-first service design and support models to improve stability, reduce manual effort, and increase operational efficiencyIdentify and implement practical AI-enabled and data-driven capabilities that enhance IT operations, user productivity, support quality, and service insightIntroduce proactive monitoring, predictive analytics, and continuous improvement practices to reduce recurring issues and improve service outcomesSecurity, Risk & Compliance

Embed security-by-design principles into endpoint, desktop application, and digital workplace services in partnership with Security leadershipEnsure services meet Firm requirements for compliance, endpoint protection, patching, configuration standards, and secure accessSupport the maturation of Zero Trust-aligned controls and secure operational practices across the user technology estateDigital Employee Experience & Service Quality

Measure and improve digital employee experience across devices, applications, and workplace services using meaningful operational and experience-based metricsOwn service quality, resilience, and continuous improvement for digital workplace and desktop application servicesDrive root-cause reduction, standardization, self-service, and automation to improve stability and reduce service frictionLeadership & Organizational Development

Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing global team, including managers and technical professionals, with clear accountability for performance and outcomesBuild organizational capability in modern endpoint management, cloud services, automation, agile delivery, and service excellenceCreate strong operating rhythms, role clarity, and cross-team collaboration to support delivery at scaleVendor, Financial & Portfolio Management

Manage vendors, contracts, licensing, and budgets across the digital workplace, endpoint, and desktop application portfolioOptimize spend, commercial performance, and service value while maintaining resilience and user experienceProvide leadership visibility into priorities, risks, service performance, roadmap progress, and investment needsQualifications, Experience and Personal Attributes

Education/Training/Certifications:

Professional Experience:

10+ years of progressive IT experience across end-user computing, digital workplace, desktop engineering, productivity platforms, or related enterprise technology domainsSignificant people leadership experience, including leading managers and technical teams in a global, multi-location environmentDemonstrated experience leading large-scale transformation of desktop, endpoint, and workplace technologies toward cloud-based, automated, and service-oriented operating modelsProven experience partnering across infrastructure, security, platform, support, and architecture teams to deliver integrated technology solutionsExperience operating in a complex, high-expectation professional services environment preferredTechnical Skills:

Deep understanding of enterprise endpoint management, desktop application delivery, and modern digital workplace servicesStrong knowledge of Microsoft end-user and cloud technologies, including Intune, Microsoft 365, Azure Virtual Desktop, Windows 365, and related management and automation capabilitiesExperience with software deployment, endpoint lifecycle automation, virtual desktop technologies, and secure remote access platformsStrong understanding of endpoint security principles, configuration standards, patching, compliance, and operational resilienceFamiliarity with digital employee experience, monitoring, observability, and service management practicesPerformance Traits:

Executive presence and strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively across senior leadership, peers, technical teams, and vendorsStrong strategic thinking, judgment, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to balance innovation, resilience, standardization, risk, and user experienceAbility to lead through change, influence without direct authority, and align diverse teams around common outcomesStrong customer and service orientation, with the ability to anticipate needs and exercise sound independent judgmentAbility to work under pressure, manage shifting priorities, and deliver against deadlines in a demanding global environmentStrong attention to detail, organizational skills, confidentiality, and discretionContinuous improvement mindset with the courage to challenge legacy practices constructivelyManagement Accountabilities

Manages processes for direct reports in regard to performance appraisals, annual compensation, goal setting, coaching, and performance counselingAccountable for organizational design, talent development, succession planning, and recruitment for the functionOwns operational budget analysis, recommendations, vendor oversight, and commercial decision supportEstablishes priorities, governance, and effective delegation to improve workflow efficiency and service delivery outcomesPhysical Requirements

May require occasional lifting of up to 20 lbs.May require travel to other offices as needed

We offer competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits, including medical/dental/vision/life/and AD&D insurance, 401(k) savings plan, back-up childcare and eldercare, generous paid time off (PTO), as well as opportunities for professional development and growth.

We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodations to applicants and employees with disabilities and disabled veterans. To request an accommodation related to the application process or interview, please email uslateralrecruiting@mayerbrown.com. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status.

Intermediate Level

jobs.title: Business Systems Engineer
jobs.summary: At Lyft, community is what we are and it’s what we do. It’s what makes us different. To create the best ride for all, we start in our own community by creating an open, inclusive, and diverse organization where all team members are recognized for what...
jobs.description:
At Lyft, community is what we are and it’s what we do. It’s what makes us different. To create the best ride for all, we start in our own community by creating an open, inclusive, and diverse organization where all team members are recognized for what they bring.

Our core corporate functions (Finance, Supply Chain) are critical to Lyft’s success. The health and sustainability of our applications, systems, tooling and processes are critical for daily operations and for Lyft’s ability to grow. This Technical Business Systems Engineering role is responsible for supporting and sustaining Lyft's corporate systems through integration management, performance monitoring, incident response, automated workflows, and robust alerting. We are seeking an experienced Oracle Fusion ERP Technical Engineer with 4 - 5 years of hands-on expertise who can operate at the intersection of Technology, and Business Operations - developing, and optimizing the Oracle Fusion Cloud technical solutions that drive Lyft's financial ecosystem. The ideal candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, analytical, and service-focused, with the technical depth and business acumen to effectively partner with stakeholders across the organization.

Your Key Responsibilities:

- Develop robust and scalable solutions using Oracle Fusion technical tools including OTBI, BI Publisher, FBDI, REST/SOAP APIs, and Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC).

- Possess extensive experience in the technical components, configurations, and underlying processes of core Oracle Fusion Financial & Supply Chain modules.

- Build and maintain integrations between Oracle Fusion Financials and boundary applications such as tax engines, billing platforms, banking systems, and payment gateways.

- Develop custom reports, dashboards, and analytics using OTBI, BICC, BI Publisher, and Oracle Fusion Data Intelligence (FDI) to meet finance and operations reporting needs.

- Extensive experience in writing and optimizing SQL and PL/SQL scripts for data extraction, transformation, and validation.

- Experienced in partnering closely with Functional Finance teams to provide hands-on technical support during time-sensitive month-end and period-close activities, ensuring timely resolution of issues, data accuracy, and seamless execution of close processes.

- Exposure to Oracle AI Agent Studio with hands-on experience developing intelligent automations and AI-driven workflows.

- Build and maintain bank transmissions and other out-of-box Oracle transmission formats, working directly with banking partners to ensure seamless integration.

- Apply thorough knowledge of Host-to-Host (H2H) connections, including key exchange concepts, security protocols, and related connectivity standards.

- Manage Oracle Fusion Cloud environments across Development, Test, UAT, and Production, including refreshes, patch releases, quarterly updates, and version upgrades.

- Experience in supporting SOX compliance by gathering, preparing, and providing accurate and timely evidence for SOX controls, audits, and internal control assessments.

- Monitor infrastructure and application performance to enhance system resilience and business continuity.

- Proficient in creating Technical Design Documents (TDDs) and configuration workbooks, with hands-on experience supporting end-to-end testing cycles to ensure solution quality and alignment with business requirements.

- Work with Oracle Support by raising and managing Service Requests (SRs), driving issues through to effective and timely resolution.

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver efficient, scalable, and robust technical solutions.

- Demonstrate a proactive willingness to identify process improvement opportunities, and propose innovative technical solutions that bring measurable value to teams and enhance existing workflows

- Maintain thorough, up-to-date documentation of system configurations, integrations, workflows, and business requirements.

- Proficient in applying both Agile and Waterfall project methodologies across development, testing, and release activities, with the ability to adapt to the delivery model best suited to the project requirements.

Skills & Attributes:

- 4 - 5 years of work experience in IT systems with deep technical knowledge of Oracle Fusion Cloud Financial & Supply Chain modules.

- Enthusiastic about learning new technologies and staying current with emerging tools

- Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills with the ability to exercise mature judgment in complex and ambiguous situations.

- Collaborative team player who thrives in cross-functional environments, fosters a culture of knowledge sharing, and contributes positively to team dynamics and collective success.

- Self-motivated and driven professional with a strong sense of ownership, proactively identifying opportunities for improvement and seeing them through to completion with minimal supervision.

- Good understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) with solid familiarity across development, support, and release management activities.

- Experience transforming data and automating processes using scripting languages such as Python, with a focus on reducing manual effort and improving operational efficiency.

Please submit your resume in English.

jobs.title: Business Systems Engineer, Oracle EPM
jobs.summary: At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive. Our core corporate finance functions are critical to Lyft’s success. The health and...
jobs.description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Our core corporate finance functions are critical to Lyft’s success. The health and sustainability of our applications, systems, tooling and processes are critical for daily operations and for Lyft’s ability to grow. The Oracle EPM Engineer possesses both business and technical acumen. This engineering role provides support for Lyft’s Corporate consolidations systems, managing their daily performance and incidents, creating automated workflows, building customer solutions, configuring applications, providing end user training and protecting all system related information assets. In this pivotal role, you will leverage your deep expertise in FCCS, ARCS, Data Exchange and EDMCS to drive innovation across our financial consolidation, reporting, reconciliation, and planning processes. Working at the intersection of finance, technology, and strategy, you'll collaborate with key stakeholders to architect solutions that enhance accuracy, efficiency, and compliance while positioning our organization for continued growth and adaptability in a dynamic business environment.

Responsibilities:

- Strategic Leadership & Expertise

- Lead all aspects of the front-end administration of the FCCS, ARCS, Data Exchange, EDMCS systems including maintaining metadata, rules, forms, developing reports, creating locations, mappings, and granting user access.

- Serve as the support person and subject matter expert for FCCS, Data Exchange and ARCS configurations, setting technical standards and driving continuous improvement initiatives

- Strong understanding of complex EPM implementation projects from conception to completion, ensuring on-time, within-budget delivery while exceeding stakeholder expectations

- Advanced System Configuration & Development

- Design and implement sophisticated FCCS business rules, including translation override rules, complex intercompany eliminations, and custom calculation rules that automate and streamline financial consolidation processes. Develop reports, books and bursting definitions in Management and Narrative reporting

- Architect advanced ARCS reconciliation frameworks with customized profiles, data loads and automated workflows that significantly reduce manual effort and improve accuracy

- Manage the integration of Oracle EDMCS with Oracle ERP Cloud, EPM Cloud, and other enterprise systems to ensure seamless data flow across the organization.

- Integration & Performance Optimization

- Design and support seamless data integration between EPM modules and enterprise systems, ensuring reliable, automated data flows that maintain data integrity.

- Proactively monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize system performance, implementing innovative solutions that enhance scalability and processing efficiency

- Lead technical issue resolution across the EPM landscape, applying advanced troubleshooting methodologies to identify root causes and implement sustainable solutions

- Stakeholder Partnership & Knowledge Leadership

- Partner closely with Finance and Accounting leadership to translate complex business requirements into effective technical solutions that drive measurable improvements.

- Develop comprehensive documentation and knowledge transfer protocols that enhance organizational understanding and adoption of EPM solutions

Experience:

- Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field; Master's degree preferred

- 5+ years of progressive experience with Oracle EPM applications, demonstrating depth in FCCS and ARCS implementation and administration

- Proven expertise in designing and deploying enterprise-scale consolidation applications, reconciliation frameworks, and data integration solutions

- Strong understanding of EDMCS architecture and governance principles with practical implementation experience

- Advanced knowledge of financial accounting principles related to consolidation and close processes.

- Experience with SQL, Groovy scripting, EPM Automate or equivalent technical languages for custom solution development

- Advanced Skills

- Exceptional analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with demonstrated ability to architect complex solutions for enterprise-level challenges

- Superior communication and interpersonal skills, adept at conveying technical concepts to diverse audiences including executive leadership

- Strong project management discipline with track record of successful, on-time deliveries in complex, matrix environments

- Ability to anticipate future business needs and proactively design scalable solutions that accommodate organizational growth

- Preferred Attributes

- Oracle EPM Cloud certifications

- Experience implementing and administering FCCS, ARCS and EDMCS for large, publicly traded companies

- Experience with Oracle applications (ERP Cloud, BI, Data Management)

- Background in multinational, multi-currency financial environments

- Experience in regulated industries with complex compliance requirements

Benefits:

- Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits

- Mental health benefits

- Family building benefits

- Child care and pet benefits

- Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account

- RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future

- In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service

- Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.

- Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $102,000 - CAD $127,500, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

This job fills an existing vacancy.

To effectively support our business stakeholders, candidates for this role must be proactive, detail oriented, highly subject oriented, analytical, service focussed and possess the ability to execute the following skills.

Your Key Responsibilities:

- Serve as a skilled Oracle Fusion Order-to-Cash (O2C) Techno-Functional professional, acting as a strategic bridge between business and technical teams to support and continuously enhance ERP Financials, O2C lifecycle, and Manufacturing modules within Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications or Oracle R12.

- Apply functional expertise alongside hands-on technical knowledge in integrations, reporting, and issue resolution, delivering results in a dynamic, client-facing environment.

- Support end-to-end O2C processes encompassing Customer & Account Setup, Order Management, Pricing & Discounts, Shipping & Fulfillment, Billing & Invoicing, Receivables & Cash Applications, Lockbox Functionality, and Collections & Adjustments.

- Bring a minimum of 4 years of hands-on implementation experience across a combination of Oracle Financials and Supply Chain modules, including Accounts Receivables, Plan to Manufacture, General Ledger, Subledger Accounting, Cash Management, and Order to Cash.

- Experience in subscription and usage-based billing environments, with an understanding of integrations with CRM, billing, and tax platforms can be an added advantage.

- As a Business Systems Engineer, you are responsible for capturing, analyzing and estimating customer business requirements and translating them into Oracle Cloud ERP and Boundary apps solution architecture that fits within the business objectives.Must be able to quickly engage in calls with the Business and stakeholders to provide immediate support during critical periods, such as the month-end close process or any other high-priority task.

- Demonstrate a strong drive to learn and investigate in the event of urgent and novel issues.

- Should be capable of coordinating the design, interpreting requirements, and translating them into ERP configurations, with expertise in Oracle Fusion setups.

- Should be completely hands on and have the ability to identify issues and track them to closure.

- Maintain good level knowledge of Accounting and Basic Business Process Flows, with solid exposure to data migration using FBDI (File-Based Data Import).

- Leverage SQL, PL/SQL, BI, OTBI reporting tools to drive data-driven insights and reporting solutions (preferred and added advantage).

- Coordinate and manage design, development, and testing activities across project phases to ensure timely and high-quality delivery.

- Show interest to Technology Innovation by staying ahead of emerging trends including Generative AI and Agentic AI to proactively identify and implement opportunities that drive O2C performance and operational efficiency.

- Drive calls with O2C Business stakeholders weekly to access and work on priorities and enhancements and get involved in month end activities during Period close.

- Work closely with Integration teams and other boundary applications to build, test, and deploy seamless connections with CRM,platform, and other supporting systems.

- Communicate effectively with diverse stakeholders through verbal, written, and presentation skills, utilizing tools such as Google Slides and Lucidchart.

- Apply problem-solving and troubleshooting capabilities with judgment to resolve technical and functional challenges within O2C.

- Maintain comprehensive and up-to-date documentation of system requirements, configurations, and processes in line with organizational standards.

- Oversee the deployment of new software releases, version upgrades, patch updates, and critical incident management.

- Experience in supporting SOX compliance by gathering, preparing, and providing accurate and timely evidence for SOX controls, audits, and internal control assessments.

- Work with Oracle Support by raising and managing Service Requests (SRs), driving issues through to effective and timely resolution.

- Manage stakeholder expectations and develop detailed, actionable implementation plans to support successful project delivery.

- Apply Agile methodology across software development, systems integration, and application configuration activities to drive iterative and efficient delivery.

- Maintain thorough, up-to-date documentation of system configurations, integrations, workflows, and business requirements.

Skills & Attributes:

- Brings 4-5 years of IT systems experience with domain expertise in Order to Cash processes within Oracle Fusion Cloud, demonstrating a strong command of functional and technical(good to have) aspects of the saas platform..

- Has a good working understanding of the software development lifecycle, with growing familiarity in development, enhancement, and day-to-day support activities.

- Should be comfortable enough to report to a Senior level Business Systems Engineer.

- A reliable and collaborative team member who works effectively in cross-functional settings, actively participates in knowledge sharing, and contributes constructively to team goals and a positive team culture.

- Should show the urge to learn new things and easily adaptable and scalable to new technologies.

- Adapt at bridging the gap between business and technology comfortable engaging empathetically with stakeholders across all levels, from Support business owners to technical partners, ensuring needs are accurately captured and clearly communicated.

Please submit your resume in English.

- Brings 4-5 years of IT systems experience with domain expertise in Source To Pay processes within Oracle Fusion Cloud, demonstrating a strong command of both functional and technical aspects of the platform.

- Has a good working understanding of the software development lifecycle, with growing familiarity in development, enhancement, and day-to-day support activities.

- A reliable and collaborative team member who works effectively in cross-functional settings, actively participates in knowledge sharing, and contributes constructively to team goals and a positive team culture.

- Should show the urge to learn new things and easily adaptable and scalable to new technologies.

- Adapt at bridging the gap between business and technology comfortably engaging empathetically with stakeholders across all levels, from Support business owners to technical partners, ensuring needs are accurately captured and clearly communicated.

Please submit your resume in English.

jobs.title: Business Systems Engineer, Tax Technology
jobs.summary: At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive. Lyft connects people to transportation to change the way we live and get around our...
jobs.description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Lyft connects people to transportation to change the way we live and get around our communities. Lyft’s engineering team is growing rapidly, and we are looking for Technical Program Managers to help us scale. Come be part of a new team at Lyft focused on enabling and empowering engineering teams to deliver at scale.

The Finance Technology team is seeking a Business Systems Engineer focused on Tax Technology to join our Boundary Applications team. In this role, you will serve as the primary owner of our tax determination and compliance systems, working at the intersection of tax policy, system architecture, and Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP integration. You will partner closely with our Oracle Engineering team and collaborate with stakeholders across Tax, Finance, Legal, and Procurement to ensure Lyft's tax systems are accurate, scalable, and audit-ready as we grow internationally. This is a high-impact opportunity to shape our global tax technology architecture during a critical period of expansion.

Responsibilities:

- Own the end-to-end configuration, maintenance, and optimization of Lyft's tax determination platform (currently Vertex), including taxability rules, product mappings, jurisdiction configurations, and rate updates

- Evaluate and implement enhancements to the current tax technology stack to meet evolving global compliance requirements, including assessing alternative or complementary solutions where gaps exist

- Design and maintain integrations between tax systems and Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP, ensuring accurate tax determination across procure-to-pay, order-to-cash, and intercompany flows

- Manage and optimize Vertex tax engine settings for rental and lease transactions, ensuring accurate tax determination based on rental length, car-specific surcharges, and drop-off location requirements

- Translate complex international tax requirements, including VAT, GST, and digital services taxes across Europe, APAC, and Latin America, into precise system configurations and taxability rules

- Own Lyft's e-invoicing compliance program across applicable jurisdictions: design and maintain the technical architecture for generating, transmitting, and archiving government-compliant electronic invoices, including clearance model integrations (where invoices must be validated by a tax authority in real time before issuance) and post-audit model reporting (e.g., SAF-T, MTD in the UK, Italy, and equivalent frameworks in APAC and LatAm)

- Partner with the Oracle and other internal Engineering team and to drive integration architecture decisions, resolve data flow issues, and ensure sales, rental and use tax and e-invoicing logic is correctly applied across ERP subledgers and boundary applications

- Monitor regulatory changes across active and emerging jurisdictions, including evolving EU ViDA (VAT in the Digital Age) mandates and country-specific e-invoicing rollouts, and proactively implement system updates ahead of compliance deadlines

- Lead troubleshooting and resolution of tax calculation errors, integration failures, and audit findings across the tax systems ecosystem

- Develop and maintain thorough documentation of tax configurations, integration designs, and control evidence to support internal audit and SOX compliance

- Provide expert guidance and training to Finance and Tax stakeholders on system capabilities, limitations, and best practices

- Actively contribute to Boundary Applications team processes and engineering culture through knowledge sharing and technical mentoring

Experience:

- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Finance, Accounting, or a related field

- 4+ years of experience in tax technology or financial systems engineering roles

- 3+ years of hands-on configuration experience with a major tax determination platform (Vertex strongly preferred; experience with Avalara, Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE, Sovos, or similar solutions also valued)

- 3+ years of Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP experience with demonstrated understanding of tax integration points across AP, AR, and GL modules

- Proven experience configuring tax rules for multiple international jurisdictions, including VAT (EU/UK), GST (APAC), and/or US sales and use tax

- Experience with at least one full-cycle implementation of a tax engine integrated with Oracle ERP or a comparable enterprise financial system

- Hands-on experience implementing or operating e-invoicing solutions in at least one country with a mandatory government reporting framework; working knowledge of both clearance model (real-time tax authority validation) and post-audit model (periodic structured reporting) approaches, and the technical differences between them

- Working knowledge of at least two of the following frameworks: EU VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA), Italy FatturaPA, Spain SII, UK Making Tax Digital (MTD), SAF-T (Poland, Portugal, Norway or equivalent), India GST e-invoicing, Mexico CFDI, or Brazil NF-e/NFS-e

- Strong understanding of procure-to-pay and order-to-cash financial processes and how tax determination intersects with each

- Experience in API development or system integrations, with ability to review and contribute to integration designs

- Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate tax law and business requirements into technical system configurations

- Experience in a SOX-regulated environment and familiarity with ITGC controls around financial systems

- Excellent communication skills; comfortable presenting technical designs and compliance impacts to both technical teams and non-technical finance stakeholders

- Experience with boundary applications such as Concur, Zip HQ, Gtreasury, or UiPath is a plus

Benefits:

- Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits

- Mental health benefits

- Family building benefits

- Child care and pet benefits

- Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account

- RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future

- In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service

- Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.

- Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $102,000 - CAD $127,500, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

This job fills an existing vacancy.

jobs.title: Staff Client Platform Engineer
jobs.summary: Who we are Aurora’s mission is to deliver the benefits of self-driving technology safely, quickly, and broadly. The Aurora Driver will create a new era in mobility and logistics, one that will bring a safer, more efficient, and more accessible future to...
jobs.description:
Who we are
Aurora’s mission is to deliver the benefits of self-driving technology safely, quickly, and broadly.

The Aurora Driver will create a new era in mobility and logistics, one that will bring a safer, more efficient, and more accessible future to everyone.

At Aurora, you will tackle massively complex problems alongside other passionate, intelligent individuals, growing as an expert while expanding your knowledge. For the latest news from Aurora, visit aurora.tech or follow us on LinkedIn.

What we are looking for
Aurora hires talented people with diverse backgrounds who are ready to help build a transportation ecosystem that will make our roads safer, get crucial goods where they need to go, and make mobility more efficient and accessible for all.

We're searching for a Staff Client Platform Engineer to join our Enterprise Site Reliability (SRE) team to lead the management of our enterprise Windows, Mac, and Ubuntu clients. The team is responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining scalable and secure client device infrastructure. They also automate device deployment, configuration, and management processes.

This role will contribute to delivering the benefits of self-driving by leading the development of tailored client device solutions that address Aurora-wide and team-specific requirements for software and hardware development and manufacturing.

In this role you will

Guide the approach and execution of the Client Platform Engineering Team
Plan, design, and implement scalable solutions to manage Aurora's physical and virtual client platforms (Macs, Windows, Ubuntu) to ensure they receive security patches, configuration, and software package updates
Partner with IT Support, InfoSec, and Developer Experience on strategy and implementation of client platform management solutions
Handle escalations from IT Support, with an emphasis on mentoring and empowering IT Support to address similar problems
Participate in the Enterprise SRE 24/7 on-call rotation

Required qualifications

Experience of at least 8 years in Client Platform engineering and at least 3 years in leading engineering solutions (design to implementation) at scale.
Expertise with client device lifecycle management, including experience across multiple client operating systems and various hardware platforms.
Experience with MacOS, Windows, and Linux system administration.
Experience deploying configuration management tools such as Intune, Kandji, Puppet, Ansible, or Chef.
Experience with automation and tool development, preferably using Powershell, Go, or Python.
Strong change management skills, including rollout planning and communication, and collaborating with stakeholders.
Understanding of client endpoint security concepts and experience applying security hardening to client devices, such as CIS Benchmarks.
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills that drive executional impact at scale.
Experience with automating testing for imaging, provisioning, and configuration management.

Desirable qualifications

Experience working within cloud environments such as AWS, GCP, or Azure.
Experience in environments deploying containerized workflows, using tools such as Docker or Kubernetes.
Familiarity with SRE concepts such as SLIs, SLOs, SLAs, incident response, and best practices and partnering with teams on them.
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, Pulumi, or CloudFormation

The base salary range for this position is $163,000 - $236,500 per Year. Aurora’s pay ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, the successful candidate’s starting base pay will be determined based on factors including job-related skills, experience, qualifications, relevant education or training, and market conditions. These ranges may be modified in the future. The successful candidate will also be eligible for an annual bonus, equity compensation, and benefits.

Working at Aurora
At Aurora, we bring together extraordinarily talented and experienced people united by the strength of our values. We operate with integrity, set outrageous goals, and build a culture where we win together — all without any jerks.

We believe in-person work increases collaboration, empathy and our ability to lead effectively. As a result, we operate in a hybrid work environment where Aurorans are in office at least 3 days per week.

Our Careers page provides insight into what it is like to work at Aurora, and you can find all the latest updates in our Newsroom.

Our commitment to safety
At the core of everything we do is our commitment to safety. Building best-in-class self-driving technology will take time, and we believe that each employee at Aurora has a role in contributing to safety, every step of the way. Aurora expects commitment to our safety policies from every employee, and seeks candidates who take an active responsibility, can contribute to building an atmosphere of trust, and invest in the organization’s long-term success by prioritizing working safely, no matter what.

Our commitment to inclusion
Aurora considers candidates without regard to their race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy status, parent or caregiver status, ancestry, political affiliation, veteran and/or military status, physical or mental disability, or any other status protected by federal or state law. Aurora considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state, and local law. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at careersiteaccommodations@aurora.tech.

For California applicants, information collected and processed as part of your application and any job applications you choose to submit is subject to Aurora’s California Employment Privacy Policy.

jobs.description:
Introduction
Do you have the career opportunities as a Senior Systems Engineer you want with your current employer? We have an exciting opportunity for you to join HCA Healthcare which is part of the nation's leading provider of healthcare services, HCA Healthcare.

Benefits
HCA Healthcare offers a total rewards package that supports the health, life, career and retirement of our colleagues. The available plans and programs include:

Comprehensive medical coverage that covers many common services at no cost or for a low copay. Plans include prescription drug and behavioral health coverage as well as free telemedicine services and free AirMed medical transportation.
Additional options for dental and vision benefits, life and disability coverage, flexible spending accounts, supplemental health protection plans (accident, critical illness, hospital indemnity), auto and home insurance, identity theft protection, legal counseling, long-term care coverage, moving assistance, pet insurance and more.
Free counseling services and resources for emotional, physical and financial wellbeing

401(k) Plan with a 100% match on 3% to 9% of pay (based on years of service)

Employee Stock Purchase Plan with 10% off HCA Healthcare stock

Family support through fertility and family building benefits with Progyny and adoption assistance.

Referral services for child, elder and pet care, home and auto repair, event planning and more

Consumer discounts through Abenity and Consumer Discounts

Retirement readiness, rollover assistance services and preferred banking partnerships

Education assistance (tuition, student loan, certification support, dependent scholarships)

Colleague recognition program

Time Away From Work Program (paid time off, paid family leave, long- and short-term disability coverage and leaves of absence)

Employee Health Assistance Fund that offers free employee-only coverage to full-time and part-time colleagues based on income.

Learn more about Employee Benefits

Note: Eligibility for benefits may vary by location.

Our teams are a committed, caring group of colleagues. Do you want to work as a(an) Senior Systems Engineer where your passion for creating positive patient interactions is valued? If you are dedicated to caring for the well-being of others, this could be your next opportunity. We want your knowledge and expertise!

Job Summary and Qualifications

Job Summary: Primary Purpose of the job

The Enterprise Endpoint Services Senior Systems Engineer plays a critical role in delivering a scalable, high-performing software management platform for over 300,000 workstations across HCA. This position focuses on automating enterprise software delivery using PowerShell and System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM), ensuring consistent application deployment, compliance, and operational efficiency. This position also plays a key role in helping the team to support the SCCM infrastructure environment.

This person must be a positive, self-starter; possessing strong communication skills effectively focused on improving our customers' experience. They must possess an advanced ability to troubleshoot and dissect complex problems with strong communication skills that connect with all customer levels while collaboratively working with many other IT&S Communities across the HCA Enterprise. This person is called on to lead projects and operational initiatives for the department within the realm of automating software delivery solutions for enterprise applications.

Responsibilities:

Enterprise Software Delivery

• Develop, test, and deploy enterprise software packages using PowerShell App Deployment Toolkit (PSADT).

• Create and maintain SCCM applications and packages for automated software deployment across large-scale environments.

• Ensure consistent and reliable delivery of all automated application solutions.

• Collaborate with application owners to gather requirements and validate deployment success.

• Maintain version control and documentation for all deployment scripts and packages.

• Utilize custom test environments to perform proof of concept implementations before moving to pilot/production availability.

PowerShell Scripting & Automation

• Design and optimize PowerShell scripts to automate software installations, configurations, and post-deployment tasks.

• Troubleshoot and enhance existing PSADT scripts for improved reliability and performance.

• Implement logging, error handling, and rollback mechanisms in deployment scripts.

SCCM Infrastructure Support

• Assists the team in ongoing support and maintenance of SCCM infrastructure including site servers, distribution points, management points, and SQL databases.

• Assists the team in performing routine health checks, capacity planning, and performance tuning of SCCM components.

• Assists the team in management of boundary groups, client settings, and site configurations to ensure optimal client communication.

• Assists the team in applying SCCM updates and hotfixes, and coordinate with Microsoft support when needed.

General Solution Support

• Utilize SCCM dynamic collections, advertisements, queries, and reports to gather necessary endpoint reporting data.

• Generate query-based collections and SCCM reports using SQL and WQL.

• Contribute to Endpoint Services Dashboard content via Splunk or other data analytics engine to provide business intelligence on the endpoint environment.

• Facilitate, Implement & Support changes to the Edge Compatibility Mode sites.xml list.

• Create, Maintain and Support the centralized management of enterprise web browsers (e.g., Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge)

• Discover, Test and Coordinate the implementation of windows configuration standards via Windows Group Policy settings.

Team Collaboration

• Collaborate with key stakeholders (Business and IT&G Leaders, vendors, and other product owners) by leading discussions and actions to drive enterprise software automation delivery, problem resolution, project milestone achievement, and produce business enhancing software delivery solutions.

• Coordinates sharing of best practices and policies and procedures.

• Participate in activities which improve departmental and organizational performance

• Adheres to and supports HCA ITG standards, policies, and procedures.

• Maintains and protects confidentiality regarding all aspects of patient care.

• Adheres to Code of Conduct and Mission and Value Statement.

Other Qualifications:

• 5+ years’ scripting and automating software delivery solutions using PowerShell with tools such as PowerShell App Deploy Toolkit.

• 5+ years’ Systems Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) Administration experience and utilizing centralized systems management systems to administer and deliver software to endpoints in an enterprise sized environment.

• 3+ years’ experience supporting Windows Operating Systems

• Advanced working knowledge of Windows 11

• Advanced working knowledge Windows Group Policy and Windows Registry for configuration of platform standards.

• Willingness to develop and expand expertise in Microsoft Intune, including endpoint configuration, device compliance policies, and application lifecycle management is required.

• Microsoft SCCM or Intune Certification and/or Knowledge Level is preferred.

Physical Demands / Working Conditions:

Prolonged sitting. Local travel in the Metro Nashville area; very limited overnight. On call availability.

HCA Healthcare has been recognized as one of the World's Most Ethical Companies® by the Ethisphere Institute more than ten times. In recent years, HCA Healthcare spent an estimated $3.7 billion in cost for the delivery of charitable care, uninsured discounts, and other uncompensated expenses.

"Bricks and mortar do not make a hospital. People do."- Dr. Thomas Frist, Sr.

HCA Healthcare Co-Founder
If you are looking for an opportunity that provides satisfaction and personal growth, we encourage you to apply for our Senior Systems Engineer opening. We promptly review all applications. Highly qualified candidates will be contacted for interviews. Unlock the possibilities and apply today!

We are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

jobs.title: SCCM Engineer
jobs.summary: Position: SCCM Engineer Contract to Hire Location: Hybrid, 4x (M-Th) Fort Lauderdale, FL Role Overview: Overview We are seeking a Senior SCCM / MECM Engineer to support and enhance our enterprise endpoint management environment. This individual will be...
jobs.description:
Position: SCCM Engineer

Contract to Hire

Location: Hybrid, 4x (M-Th) Fort Lauderdale, FL

Role Overview:

Overview

We are seeking a Senior SCCM / MECM Engineer to support and enhance our enterprise endpoint management environment. This individual will be responsible for maintaining and optimizing Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM), operating system deployment, software packaging, patch management, and endpoint lifecycle management across a large-scale Windows environment.

The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in endpoint management technologies, application deployment, Windows server administration, PowerShell automation, and enterprise systems support. This is a highly hands-on engineering role requiring both operational support and continuous improvement of the endpoint management ecosystem.

Key Responsibilities

Administer and maintain Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM) infrastructure.
Design, implement, and optimize software deployment and application packaging solutions.
Manage Windows workstation and server patching through MECM/SCCM and WSUS.
Create and maintain operating system deployment (OSD) task sequences.
Troubleshoot endpoint management, deployment, and patching issues.
Support Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, and related Windows infrastructure services.
Develop and maintain PowerShell scripts to automate system administration and deployment activities.
Monitor system health, performance, and compliance metrics.
Partner with infrastructure, security, and application teams to support enterprise initiatives.
Perform root cause analysis and implement long-term solutions for recurring issues.
Document processes, standards, and technical configurations.
Provide support for major upgrades, migrations, and enterprise technology projects.

Required Qualifications

5+ years of hands-on experience administering MECM/SCCM in an enterprise environment.
Strong experience with:

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM)
WSUS
Windows 10/11 endpoint management
Windows Server administration
Active Directory
Group Policy
DNS

Experience with Operating System Deployment (OSD) and task sequence management.
Experience with software packaging and application deployment.
Strong PowerShell scripting and automation skills.
Experience troubleshooting endpoint deployment and patch management issues.
Excellent communication and customer support skills.

Preferred Qualifications

Microsoft Intune and co-management experience.
Knowledge of Microsoft Endpoint Manager ecosystem.
Experience supporting hybrid on-premises and cloud environments.
Familiarity with vulnerability remediation and endpoint security practices.
Experience working in large enterprise environments with thousands of endpoints.
ITIL or Microsoft certifications are a plus.

Technical Environment

MECM / SCCM
WSUS
Microsoft Intune
Active Directory
Group Policy
PowerShell
Windows Server
Windows 10/11
SQL Server
Enterprise Endpoint Management

Ideal Background

Candidates should have experience managing enterprise-scale endpoint environments, including software deployment, patch management, operating system deployments, infrastructure support, automation, and ongoing optimization of the Windows endpoint ecosystem.

jobs.summary: POSITION SUMMARY: The Associate Systems Engineer (Endpoint Technology) is an entry-level role responsible for supporting the engineering, deployment, and management of enterprise endpoint devices, including desktops, laptops, and mobile devices (e.g.,...
jobs.description:
POSITION SUMMARY:

The Associate Systems Engineer (Endpoint Technology) is an entry-level role responsible for supporting the engineering, deployment, and management of enterprise endpoint devices, including desktops, laptops, and mobile devices (e.g., Zebra handhelds). This position works closely with senior engineers to maintain secure, reliable, and efficient endpoint management platforms and contributes to the lifecycle management of end-user computing devices.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

Endpoint Device ManagementAssist in the deployment, configuration, and lifecycle management of PCs, laptops, and mobile devicesSupport endpoint management platforms such as Microsoft Intune, SCCM/MECM, or other Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) toolsHelp enforce device configurations, compliance policies, and security baselinesDocumentation & Collaboration

Document procedures, configurations, and troubleshooting stepsCollaborate with IT teams including Service Desk, Infrastructure, Security, and Application teamsProvide clear communication on issue status and resolutionsAutomation & Continuous Improvement

Identify opportunities to improve endpoint deployment and management processesContribute to standardization and documentation effortsSecurity & Compliance

Support endpoint security initiatives, including antivirus, encryption, compliance enforcement, and vulnerability remediationAssist with audits and ensure devices meet corporate security standardsHelp monitor and respond to security alerts involving endpoint devicesPatching, Imaging & Deployment

Assist with OS imaging, software packaging, and application deploymentsSupport patch management processes for operating systems and endpoint applicationsOperational Support

Provide Tier 1–2 support for endpoint-related incidents, service requests, and device issuesTroubleshoot hardware, software, and connectivity issues on end-user devicesEscalate complex technical issues to senior engineers as neededParticipate in scheduled on-call or after-hours support as requiredMobile Device Support
